The declareArguments function can be modified to enhance its type inference logic. This can be achieved by checking the JSDoc parameters and default values to determine the expected types of the parameters. Additionally, the function can use the type information from the function's return type and the types of the variables used within the function to improve its type inference.

Nullability Check
A nullability check can be added to the declareArguments function to ensure that parameters with non-nullable types are not assigned null values. This can be done by adding a check for nullability when defining the slot for each parameter. If a parameter is non-nullable and is assigned a null value, a warning can be generated to alert the user of the potential bug.

The declareArguments function can be modified to generate a warning when a type mismatch is detected. This can be done by adding a check for type mismatches when defining the slot for each parameter.
